Epithelial Systems Biology Laboratory (ESBL)


Comprehensive Proteomic Analysis of Rat Renal Inner Medullary Collecting Duct Nuclei

This database reports proteins identified by mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) on the basis of at least two distinct peptides in the nuclear extract or pellet of inner medullary collecting duct (IMCD) cells freshly isolated from rat kidneys (NE-PER nuclear isolation kit, Thermo). Two samples were analyzed for each fraction, one from IMCD cells treated for 30 minutes with a vasopressin analog (dDAVP, 1 nM) and one from IMCD cells treated with the vehicle (control, CT). This database was created by Christina Pickering, Cameron Grady, Chung-Lin Chou, Pablo Sandoval, and Mark Knepper of the Systems Biology Center, Division of Intramural Research, NHLBI, NIH, Bethesda, Maryland.

Total number of proteins identified on the basis of at least two distinct peptides = 5048 (total number of proteins identified with one or more peptides was 8266). Identifications on two peptides or more in Extract and Pellet = 2669; identifications in Extract only = 1379; identifications in Pellet only = 1000.

Click on the Gene Symbol to reveal Virtual Western Blots for each protein in control samples, mapping MS data to SDS-PAGE gel slices from which they were identified.

The list of all proteins identified, including those identified on the basis of one peptide only is available here. The database of all transcription factors identified in the Extract and Pellet is available here.

Difference in spectral counts (SC) was tested using the Fisher Exact Test relative to the total spectral counts for all proteins (Extract: CT, 110787; dDAVP, 108171; Pellet: CT, 138631; dDAVP, 142352).

Download data here. Please address questions to knep@helix.nih.gov

Sort database by:

Gene Symbol Annotation Extract CT (SC) Extract dDAVP (SC) Extract dDAVP : CT Ratio Extract p-value Pellet CT (SC) Pellet dDAVP (SC) Pellet dDAVP : CT Ratio Pellet p-value
